text: Section 103 of Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023 (equivalent to old IPC Section 302): Punishment for murder. Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.

Section 303 of BNS, 2023 (equivalent to old IPC Section 378-379): Theft. Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without consent, commits theft, and shall be punished with imprisonment up to 3 years, fine, or both.

Section 85 of BNS, 2023 (equivalent to old IPC Section 498A): Husband or relative of husband subjecting a woman to cruelty shall be punished with imprisonment up to 3 years and fine. This covers dowry harassment and domestic cruelty.

Filing an FIR (First Information Report): Under Section 173 of Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023, any person can report a cognizable offence to the police, either orally or in writing, at the nearest police station, free of cost. The police are legally bound to register it (Lalita Kumari v. Govt of UP, Supreme Court, 2013 mandates FIR registration for cognizable offences).

Section 100 of BNS, 2023 (equivalent to old IPC Section 304B): Dowry death. Where death of a woman is caused by burns, bodily injury, or otherwise than under normal circumstances within 7 years of marriage, and it is shown she was subjected to cruelty for dowry, such death is dowry death, punishable with imprisonment of not less than 7 years, extending to life imprisonment.

Right to free legal aid: Under Section 12 of the Legal Services Authorities Act, 1987, women, children, SC/ST persons, persons with disabilities, industrial workmen, and persons with annual income below the state-specified limit are entitled to free legal services through District Legal Services Authorities (DLSA).
